prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response:
Imagine a brain that is much faster at doing its job than your normal human brain. The term "CPU" stands for "central processing unit." It refers to a specialized computer chip that is designed to be the main building block within any computer system to execute software that is part of the operating system, or an application like a browser or a particular game. CPUs enable the computer to perform complex mathematical calculations and process different tasks concurrently under a very tight schedule. CPUs are made out of transistors molded onto a piece of silicon that can contain hundreds of millions of these tiny circuits organized to handle tasks with incredible speed and accuracy. Just like your brain can do many different functions, so can a typical CPU, with the difference that its reach is much faster and handles different mathematical tasks in parallel with all the information updated in real time. In short, a CPU is the main component of a computer that processes information and performs functions that require computation or manipulation of data.(([1],13?2?4?5?6?2?7?8?9?10?11?12?14?15?16?17?18?19?20?21?)) [1]
 * [1] A CPU is designed to process and execute instructions loaded into it in a sequential and organized manner with each instruction being executed in a very short time period that it can continue to work on the next task listed in its queue
 * [2] It operates under a time-sharing or full-time equivalent concurrent task processing framework
 * [3] It has the ability to manipulate, utilize or access a large number of instructions at any moment as required or needed with any of these instructions not having a specific association with any other instructions
 * [4] Operations involve arithmetic and logical functions and mathematical operations that are executed using mathematical equations that use input from or manipulate data stored in registers commonly referred to as its register file
 * [5] It also has its own internal op1 division or multiplication subunits or lanes dedicated to these specific operations
 * [6] It is able to process these operations concurrently for multiple instances of the instruction wholly independently of each other
 * [7] Data on which it operates or the output generated by the processing of these operations is subject to branching and looping constructs and may be recorded in a register or register file for use in any subsequent instructions that are executed
 * [8] The completion time of a set of concurrently processing operations that end when a specific status or result is achieved is referred to as the latency of the CPU
 * [9] The CPU can be permanently or temporarily disabled, or its operation can be suspended or delayed for a specified period, with the suspended operations being completed after a specific status changes have been made to the CPU's operation process
 * [10] Depending on the specific application, the data stored in registers or register files is updated or accessed by the CPU in a regular sequence or loop
